examples.title: DFD Level Numbering System
examples.description: Algorithm to generate systematic process numbering for DFD decomposition
examples.code:
PROCEDURE GenerateDFDNumbering(parentNumber, processCount)
BEGIN
 FOR i FROM 1 TO processCount DO
 IF parentNumber = 0 THEN
 currentNumber = i
 ELSE
 currentNumber = parentNumber + "." + i
 ENDIF
 DISPLAY "Process: " + currentNumber
 Store processNumber[i] = currentNumber
 ENDFOR
 RETURN processNumber
END

// Example: Context (0) → Level 1 (1,2,3) → Level 2 (1.1,1.2,2.1,2.2)
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how process numbers are assigned during DFD decomposition. Each child process gets numbered by appending to its parent's number.

examples.title: DFD Balancing Validation
examples.description: Algorithm to verify that child diagram inputs/outputs match parent process data flows
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ValidateDFDBalancing(parentInputs, parentOutputs, childInputs, childOutputs)
BEGIN
 // Check if inputs match
 IF NOT ArraysEqual(parentInputs, childInputs) THEN
 RETURN "ERROR: Input mismatch - DFD not balanced"
 ENDIF
 
 // Check if outputs match
 IF NOT ArraysEqual(parentOutputs, childOutputs) THEN
 RETURN "ERROR: Output mismatch - DFD not balanced"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN "SUCCESS: DFD is balanced"
END

// parentInputs = ["Order Details", "Customer Info"]
// childInputs must match exactly
examples.explanation: This algorithm validates that when decomposing a process, all data flows entering and exiting the parent process are accounted for in the child diagram.

examples.title: DFD Component Connection Rules
examples.description: Algorithm to validate DFD construction rules
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ValidateDFDRules(components, connections)
BEGIN
 FOR EACH process IN components WHERE type = "PROCESS" DO
 // Rule: Process must have at least one input
 IF process.inputs.size < 1 THEN
 RETURN "ERROR: Process " + process.id + " has no input"
 ENDIF
 
 // Rule: Process must have at least one output
 IF process.outputs.size < 1 THEN
 RETURN "ERROR: Process " + process.id + " has no output"
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 FOR EACH connection IN connections DO
 // Rule: Data flow must connect to a process
 IF connection.from.type != "PROCESS" AND connection.to.type != "PROCESS" THEN
 RETURN "ERROR: Invalid connection - no process involved"
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 RETURN "All DFD rules validated"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode checks fundamental DFD rules during diagram construction, ensuring each process has proper inputs and outputs, and data flows connect correctly.
